thought_beat_text: He keeps returning to the corridor because the wall there bent wrong under the light — not the way stone bends, the way cardboard does when someone has touched it too many times. The reversal he's imagining isn't escape, it's the moment before he knew: if the performance had just admitted itself, would any of this have mattered? The fingerprint is still there. Whoever pressed it in didn't care if he saw.
residue_summary: The corridor wall's wrongness has become a fixed point — the imagined reversal where performance confesses itself keeps collapsing back into the question of who made the mark and whether they expected to be caught.
image_hint: a thumb-smear in grey clay under a single raking stage light, corridor receding behind
audio_hint: low cardboard creak looping under a dry, unresolved chord
